figures.caption: Table 3. Comparison with Modality-specific Models. The best performance is bold and the second best is underline. ‡ denotes a statistically significant improvement (p < 0.05) over the second-best results and n.s. denotes no significant improvement.

Figure 2. Left: Overview of M-IDoL. Via information decomposition, M-IDoL optimizes two objectives: (a) the routing-consistency loss Lroute, which learns modality-separable MoE subspaces to maximize H(X|Z) for inter-modality specificity, and (b) the intra-modality contrastive loss Lcst, which promotes fine-grained discrimination within each modality to minimize H(X|Y,Z) for intra-modality diversity. Right: (a) Inter-modality Entropy Maximization (H(X|Z)) and (b) Intra-modality Uncertainty Minimization (H(X|Y, Z)).
Figure 7. Confusion-matrix of our proposed M-IDoL.

Figure 4. Comparison with unified MFMs. Error bars denote standard deviation,the center position reflects the mean performance score (↑, %). p < 0.05 indicates that our M-IDoL significantly outperforms the second-best method. Statistic results are provided in Appendix D.
Figure 5. t-SNE clusters of representations across five modalities. (a–e) modality-specific models, (f–h) unified MFMs and (i) M-IDoL.
